# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #641 | scheduler — relax read∥write frontier serialization (write_awaits_drain / read-first partition): leg-contained writer co-open by default, planner-declared observation scope as consent tier | open | — | P2 (efficiency, accuracy-preserving). The adaptive scheduler serializes a genuine read∥write antichain: a mixed frontier hits the read-first-exclusive partition (adaptive-node.js:4389) so only reads open, then the writer is held by write_awaits_drain (:4532-4535) until the gate drains. A 19-guard audit classified ONLY G1 (read-first-exclusive) + G2 (write_awaits_drain) as over-conservative; the other 17 guards are load-bearing (invariants restated in the issue). The protected invariant — closed-work observation (a read/gate must render its verdict over a tree of only closed-node work + its own scratch) — is enforced temporally today; the fix enforces it structurally/contractually. R1 (default-on, structural): relax G2 so a leg-contained writer co-opens behind live reads (mirror of #622's read-direction relaxation; reuse #596 size-1 group machinery; G4 merge_awaits_read_drain retained), gated on legCoupled + parent-clean (G5) + --parallel-safe ok (G6/G7/G13) + no live lane_group (G8); any precondition failing → today's hold, byte-identical + typed serialDegradeReason. R2 (consent-tier follow-up slice): a freeze-validated `observes: scratch` node annotation (legal ONLY on adversarial-verifier; refused on code-reviewer/security-reviewer/main-session-gate) permits a legless writer co-open ONLY when the writer's declared set ⊆ (allowband docs MINUS #547 test-consumed prose per isValidationInvisible, PV:293) ∪ own evidence — so docs/decisions/** qualifies, docs/api.md / CHANGELOG.md / README.md do NOT. KEY CORRECTION baked in: barrier-invisible ≠ observation-invisible (the allowband contains chain-verdict-affecting prose; a full-diff code-reviewer is perturbed by any concurrent write) — so the naive "isBarrierInvisible writer is safe" predicate is rejected. Note R1 does NOT unlock the motivating run (dirty parent from finalize-owned-commit makes serialization doubly load-bearing: #615 close-deadlock + leg input freshness). AC + must-not-relax list + rejected alternatives in the issue body. Cross-edition #307 (adaptive-node.js is a GENERATED aggregator, ×4 editions) + walkthrough fixtures for co-run, the merge fence under co-run, each fail-closed row, and mixed-running-set crash-reconcile. Filed by user request 2026-07-08 from a scheduler-guard audit surfaced by the #640 run. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
